title: Jessie Buckley Wins Best Actress in a Motion Picture – Drama at the 2026 Golden Globes
text: Jessie Buckley has claimed the Golden Globe for Best Performance by a Female Actor in a Motion Picture – Drama for her role in Hamnet, directed by Chloé Zhao. The category was notably competitive this year. Buckley's win came against strong contenders including Jennifer Lawrence in Die My Love, Julia Roberts in After the Hunt, Tessa Thompson in Hedda, Eva Victor in Sorry, Baby, and Renate Reinsve in Sentimental Value. According to the article, every nominee was tasked with sustaining tone, tension, and audience engagement from beginning to end. The performances were unified by their emotional responsibility rather than style or genre. Buckley delivered what the article describes as a performance of quiet devastation in the film, anchoring the narrative with emotional depth throughout. The Golden Globe win provides momentum as awards season continues. The article notes that the recognition adds momentum at a critical point in the awards calendar, suggesting Buckley's performance will remain central to ongoing industry conversations about the best acting achievements of the season.
